risk.desc: Potential thermal issues due to insufficient heat dissipation.
risk.mit: Increase the size of the heat sink or add active cooling.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment during assembly due to complex snap-fit joints.
risk.mit: Include clear assembly instructions and visual aids.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference if power and signal lines are not properly separated.
risk.mit: Ensure that power and signal lines are routed separately and shielded where necessary.

risk.desc: Risk of water ingress if seals are not properly installed.
risk.mit: Double-check seal installation during assembly and include a watertight test procedure.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to the controller board if it is not securely mounted.
risk.mit: Ensure that all standoffs and mounting points are correctly installed and torqued.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to tolerance stackup.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient service access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Ensure that the service access cutout provides adequate clearance for tools and components.

risk.desc: Risk of cable damage due to inadequate strain relief.
risk.mit: Incorporate strain relief features at every flex boundary and ensure proper routing.

risk.desc: Risk of actuator misalignment leading to reduced performance.

generated_parts.label: Flexion-Resistance Actuator x 10
generated_parts.spec: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access

requirements: Plug-in tendon brake/clutch actuator module, knuckle row mount, cable pulley, connector pocket, service access
Plug-in flexion-resistance actuator modules at knuckle row
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Per-finger linkage cartridges are removable by single pin or visible hinge access
Flexion-resistance actuator modules plug in and remain visible near knuckle row
Rigid mock assembly preserves soft-contact cues, wrist quick release, and ergonomic clearance
Finger and wrist wiring uses service loops, strain relief at every flex boundary, and a stated bend radius sized for repeated flexion cycles
